summary: Iter 73 BACKEND testing of AI Insights (super_admin only, GPT-5.2 via emergentintegrations). All 16 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_iter73_ai_insights.py PASS (16/16 = 100%). Verified: (1) AUTH GUARD — all 4 endpoints (/api/admin/ai/firms, /ask, /summary, /anomalies) return 401 with no bearer token, 401 with a bogus token, and 403 for a freshly-created employee-role OTP session; (2) GET /api/admin/ai/firms → 200 with {firms:[...]} (empty list in preview DB — acceptable per spec, and _id is properly excluded); (3) POST /api/admin/ai/ask with 'How many firms and employees do I have right now?' → 200 with non-empty reply ('- **Firms:** **0**  - **Employees (total):** **1** ...') and a non-empty session_id ('ai-user_...-<ts>'); reply is real GPT-5.2 markdown, NOT the 'AI unavailable' fallback; also verified empty-question payload returns 400; (4) GET /api/admin/ai/summary?month=2026-06 → 200 with a real executive summary starting '## 2026-06 Executive Summary (TL;DR)...'; (5) GET /api/admin/ai/anomalies → 200 with real anomaly-scan text ('No clear anomalies detected from this snapshot...'); (6) VALIDATION — /api/admin/ai/summary?month=2026 correctly returns 400 ('month must be in YYYY-MM format'); (7) SMOKE — POST /api/admin/employees/bulk-import (no token) returns 401 (not 5xx), confirming the previously-broken endpoint is back online after the IndentationError fix. Real GPT-5.2 calls confirmed via emergentintegrations LlmChat; EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is properly picked up from /app/backend/.env. Super admin's PIN was NOT touched — login used the OTP dev-code flow only. Total run time ~13s including 3 live LLM roundtrips.

minor.issue: Month-format validation is a simple length/dash check ('len==7 and month[4]==\'-\'') — accepts syntactically-shaped-but-invalid values like '2026-13', 'abcd-ef', or '9999-99' without raising 400. Not blocking (LLM downstream is tolerant), but strengthening to a regex like ^\d{4}-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])$ would prevent junk reaching _gather_snapshot.

Malformed month strings that pass the shallow endpoint check still hit int() inside a try/except that silently swallows the error and falls back to last-30-days — the API response still echoes the bad month back as-is, which could confuse the operator. Consider raising 400 from the utility on parse-fail instead of the silent fallback.

action_items: None required — all Iter 73 acceptance checks pass. Optional: tighten YYYY-MM validation to reject impossible months (see backend_issues.minor).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:13378-13400 (POST /admin/ai/ask) — Correctly uses require_super_admin_strict (blocks employee, company_admin, and sub_admin, only super_admin passes). Empty question returns 400 as expected. LLM errors are wrapped in a 502 with 'AI backend error: ...' — good defensive framing.

server.py:13403-13419 (GET /admin/ai/summary) — Month validation is minimal (see minor issues). Otherwise clean. Session-id is deterministic per (user, month, cid) which is a nice choice for reproducibility.
server.py:13422-13435 (GET /admin/ai/anomalies) — Session-id includes a timestamp so each call is a fresh conversation — appropriate for a scan use-case.
server.py:13438-13447 (GET /admin/ai/firms) — Correctly excludes _id via projection; sorted by name; returns a bounded 500-doc list.

utils/ai_insights.py:141-148 — Falls back to a fixed 'AI unavailable — EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is not configured' string when the key is missing. Good defensive behaviour, though tests confirm the key IS configured in the preview .env so the fallback is not exercised in production preview.

utils/ai_insights.py:44-128 — _gather_snapshot builds an aggregate-only snapshot (headcount, punch counts, gross/net totals, ticket & leave counts). Confirmed no PII (name/phone/PAN) leaks into the LLM prompt — matches the docstring's data-privacy claim.

server.py:5914 admin_employees_bulk_import — Confirmed the previously-missing 'async def' line is now present; endpoint is reachable and returns 401 without a token (no more IndentationError crashing the whole backend).
seed_data_creation: 1 fresh employee-role OTP user was created during the run (qa.iter73.emp@test.com) purely to obtain a non-super-admin token for the 4x 403-gating checks. No companies, payrolls, punches, or PII were touched. Super admin's pin_hash / pin_must_change / pin_fail_count / pin_locked_until were NOT modified — login was via the OTP dev-code flow only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AI Insights (Iter 73) backend is production-ready — all 4 endpoints + the bulk-import smoke pass. Key operational notes: (a) frontend env variable is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (not EXPO_BACKEND_URL — differs from doc convention); (b) super admin login uses OTP dev-code flow (POST /api/auth/otp/request → response contains dev_code → POST /api/auth/otp/verify), NEVER touch the super admin's PIN; (c) EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is set in /app/backend/.env and the emergentintegrations LlmChat call to openai/gpt-5.2 works reliably (~2-4s per call); (d) preview MongoDB currently has ZERO companies and only 1 employee, so any downstream UI test should be aware the firm picker will be empty and the LLM will honestly report '0 firms, 1 employee' — this is correct behaviour, not a bug; (e) the endpoint order in server.py is /ask, /summary, /anomalies, /firms — all mounted under the /api router before app.include_router(api).
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found in Iter 73 backend. The pre-existing IndentationError on line 5914 (bulk-import) that E1 fixed this session is verified resolved — /api/admin/employees/bulk-import returns 401 (not 5xx) without a token, and the backend server process is stable (no reload crashes in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log).
